title: Question 1.1
context_text: Introduced by Balister and Bollobás in 2012, $p_{\mathrm{max}}(\Z^2)$ is the supremum of those $p$ for which there exists a 1-independent bond percolation model on $\Z^2$ in which each edge is present with probability at least $p$ but which does not percolate. The square-lattice case has attracted particular interest and remains unsolved.
paper_contribution: Obtains improved upper and lower bounds: $0.555197\dots \leq p_{\mathrm{max}}(\Z^{2}) \leq 0.8457$.

title: Question 1.2
context_text: Since $p_{\mathrm{max}}(\Z^{n})$ is decreasing in $n$ (a model that does not percolate on $\Z^n$ does not percolate on any $\Z^{n-1}$ subspace), the limit as $n\to\infty$ is well-defined. Balister and Bollobás asked for this limit in 2012; the best known lower bound $4-2\sqrt{3}\approx 0.5359$ comes from a construction of Day, Falgas-Ravry, and Hancock.
paper_contribution: Significantly improves the best known upper bound, establishing $\lim_{n\to\infty}p_{\mathrm{max}}(\Z^{n})\leq 0.5847$.

title: Falgas-Ravry–Pfenninger Conjecture on equality at $4-2\sqrt{3}$
context_text: The lower bound $p_{\mathrm{max}}(\Z^{n})\geq 4-2\sqrt{3}=0.535898\dots$ for all $n\geq 2$ is established by a construction of Day, Falgas-Ravry, and Hancock. Falgas-Ravry and Pfenninger conjectured in [13] that this bound is achieved for some $n\geq 3$, implying $\lim_{n\to\infty}p_{\mathrm{max}}(\Z^n)=4-2\sqrt{3}$.
paper_contribution: Does not resolve the conjecture; the new upper bound $\lim_{n\to\infty}p_{\mathrm{max}}(\Z^{n})\leq 0.5847$ narrows the gap but remains strictly above $4-2\sqrt{3}$.
notes: Stated in prose in the introduction without a labelled environment; attributed to [13] (Falgas-Ravry and Pfenninger, 2023).

title: Problem 6.1
statement_text: For $n\geq 3$, what is the largest $p=p(n)$ for which there is a model in $\mathcal{D}_{\geq p}(Q_{n})$ under which the hypercube is always disconnected?
context_text: First posed by Day, Falgas-Ravry, and Hancock in [11]. The construction from Theorem 1.4 of [2] shows $p\geq 1/2$; the case $n=2$ (where $Q_2=C_4$) was resolved in [11] showing the maximum is exactly $p=1/2$. The case $n=3$ and beyond remain open.
paper_contribution: Restates the problem in the open problems section; no direct progress is reported.

title: Conjecture 6.2
statement_text: For $p>0.55$, among models in $\mathcal{D}_{\geq p}(Q_{3})$ the signs model minimises the probability that $Q_{3}$ is connected.
context_text: The signs model on $Q_3$ assigns each vertex independently '$+$' with probability $\theta$ and '$-$' otherwise, with an edge open iff both endpoints share the same sign (so each edge is open with probability $p=\theta^2+(1-\theta)^2$). Computational evidence strongly suggests this model minimises the connectivity probability of $Q_3$ for $p>0.55$; for smaller $p$ other models perform worse.

title: Problem 6.3
statement_text: Is it true that for $n\geq 3$, if $p$ is sufficiently close to $1$, then among models in $\mathcal{D}_{\geq p}(Q_{n})$ the signs model minimises the probability that $Q_{n}$ is connected? If so, how close to $1$ does $p$ need to be?
context_text: The authors hypothesise that for general $n\geq 3$ the signs model minimises the probability that $Q_n$ is connected when $p$ is large enough. They note that none of the optimal models for $n=2$ are signs models (except at $p=1$). A positive resolution for $Q_3$ at $p\geq 0.5720$ would yield improved bounds on $p_{\mathrm{giant}}$ and $\lim_{n\to\infty}p_{\mathrm{max}}(\Z^{n})$.

title: Falgas-Ravry–Pfenninger Conjecture on $p_{\mathrm{giant}}$
statement_text: $p_{\mathrm{giant}}=\tfrac{1}{2}$, i.e., for all $p>\tfrac{1}{2}$, under every model in $\mathcal{D}_{\geq p}(Q_{n})$ the hypercube contains a component of size at least a constant fraction of all vertices with high probability.
context_text: The threshold $p_{\mathrm{giant}}$ is the infimum of those $p$ such that every 1-independent model on $Q_n$ with edge probability at least $p$ has a giant component with high probability when $n$ is large. The best known lower bound is $1/2$, and Falgas-Ravry and Pfenninger conjectured in [13] that this is tight.
paper_contribution: Proves $p_{\mathrm{giant}}\leq 0.5847$, improving the previously known upper bound; the conjecture that $p_{\mathrm{giant}}=1/2$ remains open.
notes: Stated in prose in Section 6 without a labelled environment; attributed to [13] (Falgas-Ravry and Pfenninger, 2023).

title: Problem 6.4
context_text: The paper establishes $p_{\mathrm{giant}}\leq 0.5847$ (when each edge of the hypercube $Q_n$ is open with probability at least $0.5847$ and $n$ is large, a giant component exists with high probability). The best lower bound remains $1/2$. Falgas-Ravry and Pfenninger conjectured $p_{\mathrm{giant}}=1/2$, but the exact value is unknown.
